hypothesis: During in situ LPTEM, a real-time closed-loop control system that uses online SAM-EM trajectory statistics (MSD vs lag, velocity autocorrelation, and displacement distribution) to adaptively modulate electron-beam dose rate and solution ionic strength will reliably steer a colloidal nanoparticle ensemble toward a predefined two-dimensional hexagonal lattice within 60 seconds, with a significantly higher success rate than identical experiments run under static conditions.
